title: styphelia cordata
intro: Styphelia cordata, also known as the Tasmanian buttonbush, is a flowering shrub native to Australia and is a member of the Epacridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Styphelia cordata include relieving coughs and congestion, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Styphelia cordata typically include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or flowers in hot water or a solvent, allowing the active compounds to be extracted.
